codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the prior blocking atom-safety flaw: the override loader now uses fixed allowlists instead of interning runtime strings, rejects unsupported perspective/override/pool-tier values during config load, and the new config/CLI/reviewer tests cover both rejection and valid override behavior. I did not find any remaining blocking issue tied to the original failure, though one previously noted resolved-snapshot source-label inaccuracy remains non-blocking.

issues.description: `resolve_panel/3` still folds `state.model_pool_sources[tier]` into `entry.sources.model` for every reviewer, even when `resolve_model/3` later takes the fixed-model branch (`loader.ex:1053-1060`) and ignores the pool entirely. If an override only changes an unrelated pool tier, fixed-model reviewers can still be reported as `source: override` in `resolved_snapshot/2`, so the redacted config evidence can overstate what was actually overridden.

summary: Reviewed the original failed review, both handoffs, the fix-session transcript skeleton, the worker skill, shared-state files, and commit `45fb6a2db7a922443dd93aa32e5702016551c58a`. The prior blocking issue is resolved: runtime override enums no longer intern attacker-controlled atoms and unsupported strings now fail during config load before planner/reviewer execution. No blocking issues remain in this fix review; one non-blocking resolved-snapshot source-label accuracy issue remains.
